notes_md:
**Phút** means *minute* (a unit of time). It’s used to talk about short amounts of time.

It’s a countable noun, so you always use a number in front: *một phút, hai phút, mười lăm phút*. No classifier needed.

Often combined with **giờ** (hour) to tell time lengths or exact times.  

It can also mean “a moment” in a more emotional or dramatic way, like a special or critical moment in life.

translation.en: We will wait a few more minutes.
I will arrive in a few minutes.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I will be back in five minutes, please wait for me.
The meeting starts at eight o'clock and lasts thirty minutes.
Every morning, she spends ten minutes meditating and breathing deeply.
notes:
Phút means minute (a unit of time). It’s used to talk about short amounts of time.

It’s a countable noun, so you always use a number in front: một phút, hai phút, mười lăm phút. No classifier needed.

Often combined with giờ (hour) to tell time lengths or exact times.  

It can also mean “a moment” in a more emotional or dramatic way, like a special or critical moment in life.

example.en: I will arrive in a few minutes.
example.vi: Tôi sẽ đến trong vài phút.

definitions.en: A unit of time equal to sixty seconds.
definitions.es: Una unidad de tiempo igual a sesenta segundos.
definitions.vi: Một đơn vị thời gian bằng sáu mươi giây.

exampleSentences: Em sẽ quay lại sau năm phút, anh đợi em nhé.
Buổi họp bắt đầu lúc tám giờ và kéo dài ba mươi phút.
Mỗi sáng chị dành mười phút để thiền và thở sâu.
